question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `left join node_info n on abe.node_id = n.node_id` to determine which nodes have entries in the `node` table (non-null `adminKey`, `declineReward`) versus those that do not, leaking information about the node governance registration status of each network node?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user pass duplicate values in the `nodeIds` Long[] array (e.g., [1L, 1L, 1L, ...] with thousands of duplicates) to cause PostgreSQL to evaluate `abe.node_id = any(:nodeIds)` against a large array with redundant entries, wasting database resources without any deduplication at the repository layer?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `nodeCertHash` field returned via `convert_from(abe.node_cert_hash, 'UTF8')` to obtain certificate hash values for all network nodes, enabling certificate pinning bypass research or targeted certificate-based attacks against node TLS connections?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `stakingPeriod` field returned from `ns.staking_period` to determine the exact staking period boundaries for each node, enabling timing attacks that exploit staking period transitions to manipulate reward calculations?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send repeated rapid requests to `findNetworkNodes()` with varying `fileId` values to cause repeated execution of the `latest_address_book` CTE's `order by start_consensus_timestamp desc limit 1` subquery, exhausting database connection pool resources through query amplification?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `declineReward` boolean field returned from `n.decline_reward` to identify which nodes have opted out of staking rewards, using this information to infer node operator financial strategies or identify nodes that may be operated by specific known entities?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user pass `nodeIds` as a null Long[] (as opposed to an empty array) to the `coalesce(array_length(:nodeIds, 1), 0) = 0` check, causing `array_length(NULL, 1)` to return NULL, which `coalesce` converts to 0, effectively bypassing the node ID filter and returning all nodes regardless of intent?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `jsonb_agg(... order by abse.ip_address_v4 asc, abse.port asc)` ordering in the service endpoints subquery to infer the relative ordering of IP addresses stored in the database, leaking information about the internal IP address allocation scheme of the network?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `rewardRateStart` field (mapped from `ns.reward_rate`) to obtain precise reward rate values for all nodes, enabling calculation of expected staking returns and potentially identifying nodes with anomalous reward rates that indicate misconfiguration?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `endConsensusTimestamp` and `startConsensusTimestamp` fields returned from `ab.end_consensus_timestamp` and `ab.start_consensus_timestamp` to determine the exact lifecycle of address book versions, enabling timing-based attacks that exploit address book update windows?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `description` and `memo` fields from `abe.description` and `abe.memo` to extract operator-provided metadata that may contain sensitive information such as internal hostnames, contact details, or operational notes inadvertently stored in the address book?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `orderDirection` is a `String` parameter with no enum validation at the repository interface level — the method signature accepts any `String` — to pass case-variant strings like 'asc', 'Asc', 'ASC ' (with trailing space) that fail the CASE comparison and produce undefined sort order, breaking pagination?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `node_info` CTE which selects `grpc_proxy_endpoint` as a JSONB column from the `node` table without any filtering, to retrieve internal gRPC proxy configuration for all nodes simultaneously in a single unauthenticated API call?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of query result caching on `findNetworkNodes()` to perform cache-busting attacks by varying `fileId` or `nodeIds` parameters across rapid successive requests, forcing repeated expensive CTE evaluations that bypass any upstream caching layer?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `left join latest_node_stake ns on abe.node_id = ns.node_id` combined with `nullif(ns.max_stake, -1)` to determine that a sentinel value of -1 is used for unset stake values, leaking internal data representation conventions that could be used to craft targeted queries or understand the data model?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `fileId` parameter defaulting to 102 (as documented in the Javadoc) to always query the primary address book file, and by passing other known file IDs (e.g., 101) enumerate alternative address book files that may contain different or more sensitive node configurations?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `join latest_address_book ab on ab.start_consensus_timestamp = abe.consensus_timestamp` inner join condition to infer the exact `start_consensus_timestamp` of the latest address book by observing which node entries are returned, enabling timestamp enumeration without direct access to the `address_book` table?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `nodeAccountId` field returned via `coalesce(n.account_id, abe.node_account_id)` to enumerate all node account IDs on the network, enabling targeted account-based attacks or correlation with transaction data to identify node operator financial activity?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a PostgreSQL out-of-memory error by passing `minNodeId = 0` and `maxNodeId = Long.MAX_VALUE` with `limit` set to a very large integer, causing the database to attempt to sort and return an unbounded result set that exhausts server memory?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `serviceEndpointsJson` field — which is constructed via `jsonb_agg` and returned as a text string — to receive malformed JSON if the underlying `address_book_service_endpoint` data contains special characters, causing a `JsonProcessingException` in `NetworkNodeMapper.parseServiceEndpointList()` that leaks internal stack trace information?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `grpcProxyEndpointJson` field — constructed via `jsonb_build_object` in the SQL query — to receive a JSON object where `port` is cast via `(n.grpc_proxy_endpoint->>'port')::integer`, and if the port value exceeds PostgreSQL integer range, trigger an overflow error that causes an unhandled exception with internal error details?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@NullUnmarked` annotation on `NetworkNodeDto` — which disables null safety checks — to receive null values in fields like `nodeId`, `fileId`, or `startConsensusTimestamp` that downstream code assumes are non-null, causing NullPointerExceptions in `NetworkNodeMapper.mapTimestampRange()` or `mapStakingPeriod()` that expose internal error details?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `case when abe.node_cert_hash is null or abe.node_cert_hash = ''::bytea then '0x'` branch to determine which nodes have no certificate hash stored, identifying potentially less-secured or newly provisioned nodes that may be more vulnerable to attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `case when abe.public_key is null or abe.public_key = '' then '0x'` branch to identify nodes with missing public keys, which may indicate misconfigured or compromised nodes that could be targeted for further exploitation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `latest_address_book` CTE's `order by start_consensus_timestamp desc limit 1` to always retrieve only the most recent address book, and by comparing results across multiple calls detect when the address book is updated, enabling real-time monitoring of network topology changes without authorization?
